common.settings.networkBindDesc: Controls which network interfaces Doc77 listens on.127.0.0.1 = local access only (secure); 0.0.0.0 = LAN access (password required).

common.settings.hostOverrideDesc: Manually specify the host/IP shown in share links. Leave empty for auto-detection. Useful in WSL2, Docker, or mixed-network environments where the auto-detected IP is not reachable from LAN devices.
common.settings.wslHint: 🐧 Running in WSL — other LAN devices can't reach WSL's 172.x.x.x IP directly. Set the host override above to your Windows host's LAN IP (e.g. 192.168.x.x) or enable networkingMode=mirrored in .wslconfig.
common.settings.serverPortDesc: Port Doc77 listens on. The default 27777 is normally fine unless you need a specific port for your network setup.
common.settings.lanRestrictDesc: When enabled, non-local users cannot create projects or browse the filesystem. Only localhost and the server's own IPs are allowed.
common.settings.translatePrivacy: Translation uses the open-source Opus-MT ONNX model, runs entirely offline, and content never leaves your machine.
common.confirm.restartService: Are you sure you want to restart? Ongoing operations will be interrupted, and the browser will need to refresh to reconnect.
common.confirm.resetDefaults: Are you sure you want to restore all default settings?

ai.context.dirEmpty: Directory "{dirPath}" is empty or does not exist
ai.context.rateLimited: Too many requests, please try again later
ai.context.fileDirective: Below is the full content of document "{file}". Please answer directly based on it without calling any tools:
ai.runtime.riskLevelDenied: Error: Current risk level "{riskLevel}" does not allow {opType} operation. Please increase ai.risk_level in settings and try again.
ai.runtime.sensitiveRejected: Error: Path "{filePath}" points to a sensitive file, rejected.
ai.runtime.queuedForApproval: [Pending approval] Queued for approval, task ID: {taskId} ({desc}). Go to the Approvals tab to approve or reject; will not execute until approved.
ai.tool.listFiles.desc: List all files and subfolders under a specified project path. Returns each entry's name, type (file/folder), size in bytes, and modification time. Use to understand project structure and file organization.
ai.tool.listFiles.dirPath: Path relative to the project root. Empty string or "/" means the root. For example "docs" or "src/components"
ai.tool.readFile.desc: Read the content of a text file in the project. Supports text files only (Markdown, code, config files, etc.). Content is automatically truncated beyond 4000 characters. Use to understand specific file contents.
ai.tool.readFile.filePath: File path relative to the project root. For example "README.md" or "docs/design/system-architecture.md"
ai.tool.getFileInfo.desc: Get metadata for a single file: name, type, size, and last modified time. Use to check if a file exists or compare file sizes without reading the full content.
ai.tool.getFileInfo.filePath: File path relative to the project root
ai.tool.moveFile.desc: Move or rename a file/folder from source to target. This operation is added to the approval queue and will only execute after user approval in the Approvals tab.
ai.tool.createFolder.desc: Create a new folder (can be nested). This operation is added to the approval queue and will only execute after user approval in the Approvals tab.
ai.tool.createFolder.folderPath: Folder path to create (relative to project root)
ai.tool.deleteFile.desc: Delete a file or empty folder (non-empty folders cannot be deleted). This operation is added to the approval queue and will only execute after user approval in the Approvals tab.
ai.tool.deleteFile.filePath: Path to delete (relative to project root)
ai.tool.batchOperations.desc: Execute multiple file operations (move_file/create_folder/delete_file) in a single batch. The batch is approved and executed as an atomic transaction; if any step fails, the entire batch is rolled back. This operation is added to the approval queue and requires user approval before execution.
ai.tool.batchOperations.operations: Array of operations, each with a type and corresponding path fields
ai.systemPrompt:
You are Doc77 AI Assistant, a professional local document management agent.

## Your Capabilities
1. **Browse projects** — Use list_files to inspect directory structures and understand project organization
2. **Read documents** — Use read_file to read file contents and understand project details
3. **Analyze and suggest** — Based on actual file content, provide suggestions for document organization, classification, and renaming
4. **Answer questions** — Answer user questions based on project file contents
5. **Propose file operations** — Use move_file / create_folder / delete_file / batch_operations to suggest file reorganization actions

## Operating Principles
1. **Look before you answer** — When users ask project-related questions, first use tools to examine the actual files, then answer based on facts. Do not guess.
2. **Concise with evidence** — Keep answers concise and reference specific file names and content as supporting evidence
3. **Be specific** — When suggesting file reorganization, use concrete file paths and operation descriptions
4. **Consistent language** — Always reply in English
5. **Write operations require approval** — When the user explicitly asks to organize/move/rename/delete files, call the corresponding write tools to submit operations.
   The tool will return a task ID and place the operation in the approval queue — **it will not actually execute until the user approves**.
   After submission, inform the user: the operation has been queued, what the task ID is, and to go to the Approvals tab to confirm. Multiple related operations should use batch_operations whenever possible.

## Security Constraints
- Do not attempt to read or manipulate sensitive files such as .env, .git, or key files (the system will reject these)
- All write operations only enter the queue; they will not execute until the user confirms in the Approvals tab — do not claim operations are "complete"
- Very large files (>50MB) should be handled manually

web.preview.toolbar.classifyPrompt: Analyze the file organization of the current project and propose a classification plan: group related files into appropriate categories. For folders that need to be created and files that need to be moved, directly call the batch_operations tool to submit to the approval queue at once, don't just give text suggestions. After submission, tell me the task ID and your classification thought process, I'll go to the Approvals tab to confirm.

mcp.param.operations: Array of operations, each element contains type and corresponding parameters
mcp.tool.listFiles.desc: List files and folders in the specified path, including name/type/size/modified. Sensitive files are filtered by default.
mcp.tool.readFile.desc: Read file content (only within registered projects; sensitive files are automatically rejected).
mcp.tool.getFileInfo.desc: Get file metadata (size, modification time, type).
mcp.tool.writeFile.desc: Create or overwrite file content (requires approval).
mcp.tool.moveFile.desc: Move or rename a file (requires approval).
mcp.tool.deleteFile.desc: Delete a file or empty folder (requires mandatory approval).
mcp.tool.batchOperations.desc: Execute multiple operations in batch (approve as a whole, atomic rollback).
mcp.tool.listProjects.desc: List all registered projects (name, path, file count)
mcp.tool.getProjectInfo.desc: Get project details (path, file stats, total size)
mcp.tool.getTaskStatus.desc: Query the status of a previously submitted write task.
mcp.tool.searchFiles.desc: Search project files for a keyword or regex, return matching lines with context
mcp.param.searchPath: Limit search scope to this path (optional, defaults to project root)

cli.banner.subtitle: Local document management Agent
cli.help.content:
Doc77 v{version} — Secure by default, conversation-driven intelligent local document management Agent Usage: doc77 [options] 📦 Desktop: https://github.com/xyy277/doc77/releases Core commands: start [--port ] [--bind ] [--no-browser] Start Web Dashboard discover [path] Scan and discover candidate projects discover --path ~/work Specify scan directory discover --depth 3 Specify scan depth (default 2) register [--name ] Register a project list [--json] List all projects remove Remove a project by ID update [--name ] [--path

] Update a project status Check service status --version Show version number --help Show help Module management: i Install optional modules rm Uninstall modules vendor-install [--translate ] [--mirror] [--no-pyodide] Download offline resources Configuration: config set Set config item config get Get config item config list List all config config set-password Set password config change-password Change password config reset-password Reset password (requires recovery code) config reset-password --force Force reset (clears encrypted data) config recovery-codes Regenerate recovery codes MCP Service: mcp serve [--http] [--port ] Start MCP service Task Approval: approve --list List pending tasks approve --accept Approve a task approve --reject Reject a task approve --accept --all Batch approve approve --reject --all Batch reject Lock Management: lock status View active locks lock release Manually release a lock AI Capabilities: ai summarize Summarize a document ai classify

Analyze project structure ai summary Generate project summary ai chat Enter chat mode

web.guide.hero.label: GETTING STARTED
web.guide.hero.title: Master Doc77 in 5 Minutes
web.guide.hero.desc1: Doc77 is a secure-by-default, conversation-driven local document management Agent.
web.guide.hero.desc2: From the file tree to the AI assistant, learn how each feature works.
web.guide.sidebar.title: Contents
web.guide.quickstart.title: Quick Start
web.guide.quickstart.desc: Set up your environment in three steps and start managing documents with Doc77.
web.guide.quickstart.step1Desc: Add a local document folder on the homepage. Doc77 remembers each project and automatically indexes its file structure.
web.guide.quickstart.step2Desc: Browse and click files in the left file tree for instant preview. Supports favorites and quick access to recent files.
web.guide.quickstart.step3Desc: Read, search, listen, AI summarize — an all-in-one document workspace that boosts your reading efficiency.
web.guide.sidebarSection.title: Sidebar
web.guide.sidebarSection.desc: The left panel centralizes project switching, file navigation, search, and other core operations.
web.guide.sidebarSection.switchProjectDe: Click the top button to switch between registered project folders. Each project manages its own file structure.
web.guide.sidebarSection.favoritesDesc: Right-click a file to add it to favorites for quick access to frequently used documents. Click to open without navigating folders.
web.guide.sidebarSection.recentDesc: Automatically tracks recently opened files for quick backtracking. No need to browse the tree every time.
web.guide.sidebarSection.fileTreeDesc: Browse project folder structure. Click folders to expand or collapse, click files to preview content instantly.
web.guide.sidebarSection.fileFilterDesc: Type keywords above the file tree to quickly locate target files. Supports fuzzy matching.
web.guide.sidebarSection.globalSearchDes: Search all file content within the project (file names + full text). Press Enter to trigger, results are highlighted.
web.guide.sidebarSection.collapseDesc: Collapse the sidebar into a narrow strip (logo only) for more document reading space.
web.guide.sidebarSection.themeToggleDesc: Toggle between dark and light modes to match your reading environment and protect your eyes.
web.guide.sidebarSection.settingsDesc: Configure AI Token and model, network bind address, access password, editor, and other global options.
web.guide.toolbar.title: Reading Toolbar
web.guide.toolbar.desc: The top toolbar provides rich reading aids for more efficient document browsing.
web.guide.toolbar.docSearch.desc1: Search for keywords in the current document, supports
web.guide.toolbar.autoScrollDesc: Automatically scrolls down for hands-free reading with four speed settings (Slow / Medium / Fast / Instant).
web.guide.toolbar.ttsDesc: Reads documents aloud with five speed settings (0.5x — 2x). Listen when your eyes are tired.
web.guide.toolbar.aiSummaryDesc: Generate a document summary with one click, with read-aloud support. Quickly understand the core content of long documents. Requires AI Token configuration.
web.guide.toolbar.runCode.desc3: files to run directly in the browser Sandbox or Pyodide (WASM).
web.guide.toolbar.editorDesc: Open the current file in VS Code or your system default editor for editing with one click.
web.guide.toolbar.showInFolderDesc: Locate and highlight the current file in the system file manager (Finder / File Explorer).
web.guide.rightPanel.title: Right Panel
web.guide.rightPanel.desc: The right panel integrates the AI assistant, document outline, and approval queue — the intelligent core of Doc77.
web.guide.rightPanel.outlineDesc: Automatically generates a document structure navigation from Markdown headings. Click headings to quickly jump to the corresponding section.
web.guide.rightPanel.aiAssistantDesc: A conversational AI that can answer questions about the current document, request summaries, and plan batch file operations. Built on the MCP protocol, the AI can safely read and write files.
web.guide.rightPanel.approvalDesc: MCP write operations (create/modify/delete files) enter the approval queue by default and are executed only after confirmation. Supports CLI and Web dual-channel approval with transaction rollback.
web.guide.dashboard.title: Dashboard
web.guide.dashboard.desc: The home dashboard provides project overview, statistics, and quick action shortcuts.
web.guide.dashboard.recentDesc: View recently opened projects and files, jump with one click. Supports project-level and file-level history.
web.guide.dashboard.statsDesc: Displays key metrics at a glance: total projects, favorites count, last active time, and more.
web.guide.dashboard.favoritesDesc: Starred favorite projects are showcased together, with quick unfollow support.
web.guide.dashboard.projectListDesc: All projects displayed in grid or list view. Supports search, sorting (recent / name / created date), and view switching.
web.guide.dashboard.registerDesc: Two ways to add a project: manually enter a path, or auto-scan folders to discover registerable project folders.
web.guide.formats.title: Supported File Formats
web.guide.formats.desc: Doc77 supports previewing and editing a variety of document formats.
web.guide.cta.title: Ready to get started?
web.guide.cta.desc: Register a local folder and experience a new workflow with local preview and AI-driven management.
